other_observers: Joe Roller, Steve Larson, and maybe one or two others; the bird I saw I found and identified, though it is quite likely it was the same bird as seen by Joe and Steve earlier and later.
description: the bird was a vireo the same size and shape as nearby Warbling Vireos. The bill was a bit shorter, appearing more stubby, and was black. I did not note the color of the legs.  The bird's cap was dark gray, noticeable darker than on Warbling Vireos seen that day. It contrasted strongly with a very pale yellowish or whitish eyebrow. The bird had a dark eyeline, including in front of the eye, that also contrasted with the eyebrow. The back and wings were olive-green, though not particularly bright. The tail was the same color. The throat and upper chest was bright yellow, contrasting with a paler yellow belly and flanks.Behaviors: the bird was foraging high in a tree, and was moving through the canopy fairly fast, making it hard to see.Call: none heardPlumage: unknown
